docs.text: Fetch the working context: import the project 'rag-text-serve'. Project is a placeholder for the code, data, and management of the data operations inside the platform.

docs.title: 2.1 Deploy VLLM serve¶
docs.text: Fetch the "vllm-text-serve" operation in the project. It deploys a large language model (LLM) using the VLLM engine within the platform. For more detailed information, please refer to the catalog function LLM VLLM Serve

docs.title: Test VLLM Serve¶
docs.text: Send a test request to the deployed VLLM service to verify it is running correctly. We will use the OpenAI-compatible API endpoint to send a chat completion request with the deployed model.

docs.title: 2.2 Embedding¶
docs.text: We will now fetch a function 'embed' from the project which will convert text documents into vector embeddings for semantic search and retrieval.

docs.title: 2.3 Text Extraction¶
docs.text: Before proceeding to the generate embedding step, it is important to extract and generate an artifact from the source document in PDF document. For more detailed information, please refer to the catalog function Text Extraction Service that demonstrates how to convert a PDF document into text format, which can then be used for generating embeddings. You are free to change the address or local path to any PDF file you prefer. For the purposes of this tutorial, the function demonstrates processing a PDF file downloaded from a local PDF folder 'documents'.

docs.title: 2.4 Embedder Function¶
docs.text: Fetch the 'embedder' function from the project. This function is a Python function that will process the extracted text content from the PDF and generate vector embeddings that capture semantic meaning, enabling efficient similarity-based searches across the document corpus. The embedder function utilizes the embedding service deployed earlier to convert text into dense vector representations.

docs.title: Query the RAG Service¶
docs.text:
Now that the RAG service is deployed, you can send queries to retrieve answers based on the embedded document corpus. The service will:

  1. Embed the Query: Convert your question into vector embeddings using the deployed embedding service
  2. Retrieve Documents: Search the document corpus to find semantically similar content
  3. Generate Response: Use the deployed LLM to generate contextually accurate answers based on retrieved documents

Send a POST request to the RAG service endpoint with your question. The service will return answers grounded in the document corpus that was processed in the text extraction and embedding generation steps.

docs.title: Index
docs.text: < BrowseText Extraction Service (Project) latestRepositoryDefinitionReference ▼hub://projects/text-extraction-service:1.0.0❐✓❐NameText Extraction Service (Project)DescriptionThis project demonstrates how to deploy a text extraction service using Apache Tika in a container and a Python function to interact with it, all orchestrated within the platform.Version1.0.0Labelslicense:apache-2.0platform:0.14type:servicephase:utility UsageNotebook

docs.title: Text Extraction Service
docs.text:
This project implements a text extraction service using Apache Tika and Python. It provides a REST API endpoint that accepts POST requests containing file artifacts and extracts text content from them.

The service consists of two main components:

  • Tika Container: Apache Tika service for document text extraction
  • Python Function: Handler that orchestrates the extraction workflow, downloads artifacts, sends them to Tika, and logs the extracted output

docs.title: Extract
docs.text:
The extract function is a Python-based handler that processes document artifacts through Apache Tika for text extraction.

import requests

def extract_text(tika_url, artifact, project):
    print(f"Downloading artifact {artifact.name}...")
    fp = artifact.as_file()
    if not (tika_url)[:4] == "http": 
        tika_url = "http://"+tika_url
    print(f"Sending {fp} to {tika_url}...")    
    response = requests.put(tika_url+"/tika",headers={"Accept":"text/html"}, data=open(fp,'rb').read())
    if response.status_code == 200:
        print("Extracted text with success")
        res = "/tmp/output.html"
        with open(res, "w") as tf:
            tf.write(response.text)
        project.log_artifact(kind="artifact", name=artifact.name+"_output.html", source=res)
        return res
    else:
        print(f"Received error: {response.status_code}")
        raise Exception("Error")

Parameters:

  • tika_url: URL endpoint of the Tika service
  • artifact: The document file to be processed
  • project: Project context for logging results

Workflow:

  1. Downloads the artifact file
  2. Ensures proper HTTP URL formatting for Tika endpoint
  3. Sends the file to Tika via PUT request with Accept: text/html header
  4. On success (HTTP 200), writes extracted text to /tmp/output.html
  5. Logs the output artifact back to the project
  6. Raises exception on extraction failure

docs.title: Tika
docs.text:
The Tika service is a containerized Apache Tika instance that provides document text extraction capabilities via REST API.

Container Image: apache/tika:latest

Port: 9998

Endpoint: PUT /tika/form

Request Headers:

  • Accept: text/html: Specifies HTML output format

Response: Extracted text content from the document

Supported Formats: PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, images with OCR, and other common document types

In [ ]: 
import digitalhub as dh
PROJECT_NAME = "tika"
proj = dh.get_or_create_project(PROJECT_NAME)
In [ ]: 
tika_function = proj.get_function("tika")

Then, execute the function. For more details on how to create, run and view jobs inside the platform, see the documentation.

In [ ]: 
tika_run = tika_function.run("serve", service_ports = [{"port": 9998, "target_port": 9998}], wait=True)

As a result the 'tike' extraction service get deployed.

In [ ]: 
service = tika_run.refresh().status.service
print("Service status:", service)

Now fetch the URL of the service for API invocation.

In [ ]: 
TIKA_URL = tika_run.status.to_dict()["service"]["url"]
print(TIKA_URL)
In [ ]: 
extract_function = proj.get_function("extract")
In [ ]: 
pdf = proj.log_artifact("document.pdf",kind="artifact", source="docs/document.pdf")

Then, we run the function by passing it the artifact and the URL to Tika:

In [ ]: 
extract_run = extract_function.run("job", inputs={"artifact": pdf.key}, parameters={"tika_url": TIKA_URL}, wait=True)

Let's read the file and check the content is correct:

In [ ]: 
html_artifact = proj.get_artifact("document.pdf_output.html")
html_artifact.download()
with open('./artifact/output.html', 'r') as file:
    file_content = file.read()
    print(file_content)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
To use the text extraction service, follow these steps:

  1. Initialize the project and deploy Tika service:
import digitalhub as dh
proj = dh.get_or_create_project("tika")
tika_function = proj.get_function("tika")
tika_run = tika_function.run("serve", service_ports=[{"port": 9998, "target_port": 9998}], wait=True)
TIKA_URL = tika_run.status.to_dict()["service"]["url"]
  1. Log a document artifact:
artifact = proj.log_artifact("document.pdf", kind="artifact", source="path/to/document.pdf")
  1. Run the extraction function:
extract_function = proj.get_function("extract")
extract_run = extract_function.run("job", inputs={"artifact": artifact.key}, parameters={"tika_url": TIKA_URL}, wait=True)
  1. Retrieve the extracted output:
output_artifact = proj.get_artifact("document.pdf_output.html")
output_artifact.download()
with open('./artifact/output.html', 'r') as f:
        print(f.read())

docs.title: 1. Initialize the project¶
docs.text: Fetch the working context: import the project 'tika'. Project is a placeholder for the code, data, and management of the data operations inside the platform.

docs.title: Deploy a text extraction service¶
docs.text: Fetch the "tika" operation in the project. This function will use Apache Tika, a tool for extracting text from a variety of formats.

docs.title: Extract the text¶
docs.text: We will not fetch a python function 'extract' from the project which will read an artifact from the platform's repository and leverage the Tika service to extract the textual content and write it to a HTML file.

docs.title: Create artifact¶
docs.text: We now store the PDF file as an artifact and download it. You are free to change the address to any PDF file you prefer. For the purposes of this tutorial, the PDF file is downloaded from a local PDF folder 'documents'.
